channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: 182 trafalgar st se17 2tp, 18th April
item.description:
Constant overflowing rubbish bins and large items that won't be collected left by rubbish collectors on the side of the road. The broken mobility scooter juts out into the footpath and is a trip hazard. Please give this household another bin, this is every single week, the rubbish ends up blowing and being spread by foxes so their neighbours bear the consequences and the council end up street cleaning to fix.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 135, Trafalgar Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Corner of Mackenzie and Beckenham Road, 18th April
item.description:
Continuing issue since August 2025

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 251, Beckenham Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Concrete bollard knocked over, 18th April
item.description:
This concrete bollard was knocked over a month ago. It is now rolling around the parade pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shooters Hill Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter by bustop, 18th April
item.description:
Please could you do a sweep of St Mary’s Avenue. There’s litter near the bus stop. Thank you!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Mary's Avenue, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near Tool station green area - Eastnor Road, 18th April
item.description:
Dear Council, I would like to report a persistent fly-tipping issue on Eastnor Road. There is a large amount of dumped rubbish, including an abandoned bicycle and a shopping trolley, left right next to the green space close to Tool station. This mess has been there for 2 weeks now, and it keeps growing. Please can you arrange for it to be removed as soon as possible? It’s an eyesore and not fair on the local community.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eastnor Road, New Eltham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Metal barriers, 18th April
item.description:
There was an accident on eastern way a couple of months back, the barriers on the elevated part of the road have been replaced. The old damaged ones though have simply been left were they fell under the flyover.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Ridgeway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: FAO Bexley Council .The cycle lane road markings have faded / worn away / missing. New updates on The cycle lane road markings have worn out and some are completely missing. Needs urgent repainting for safety Your report has been investigated by our team and this is not the responsibility of Transport for London. However, your local borough will be able to help you. Should you need anything else please contact our customer services team on 0343 222 1234 State changed to: not TfL’s responsibility TfL, 18th April
item.description:
FAO Bexley Council .The cycle lane road markings have faded / worn away / missing. New updates on The cycle lane road markings have worn out and some are completely missing. Needs urgent repainting for safety Your report has been investigated by our team and this is not the responsibility of Transport for London. However, your local borough will be able to help you. Should you need anything else please contact our customer services team on 0343 222 1234 State changed to: not TfL’s responsibility TfL
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourne Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
